sections.label: Macro
sections.body: Risk-off deepened across the board. BTC fell under $74K, Fear & Greed compressed to 22, and the Iran peace-deal odds collapsed from 23.5% to 12.5% as strikes resumed. Spot ETF outflows accelerated to $733M in a single session while equities held record highs. Capital is leaving crypto, not rotating within it.

sections.label: Perps
sections.body: The derivatives tape is dead quiet. Gold is the only asset coiling and the rest sit neutral with funding near zero. XLM is the one exception, running the only real volume expansion in the universe at 2.26x with a short-squeeze structure building under a +17% day.

sections.label: Book
sections.body: The AI-compute concentration risk fired. The sector that swept the winners two days ago led the losers today — RENDER -12.5%, AKT -9.7%, EIGEN -8.7%. Closing the two weakest momentum names at their stops and riding the holds that absorbed the flush on relative strength (TAO, ICP) or a deep stop (EIGEN).

market_sentiment.bias_line: Bias · trim the compute book into the flush, hold the names showing relative strength. One idiosyncratic long on XLM against a dead tape. No broad risk-on here. This is a stock-picker's session, not a market bid.

current_positions.thesis_note: Legacy-alt bleed intact on a deepening risk-off tape, absent from the data a sixth session with no catalyst to defend the bid.
current_positions.watch: A daily close above $370 ends it. Risk-off deepening into BTC sub-$74K and breadth 5/20 keeps the structural bleed alive through the final day of the window.

current_positions.thesis_note: Took the sector hit at -8.7% but sits only 2% under entry with the stop 9% below — the most room of the compute longs.
current_positions.watch: Holding above the $0.205 stop with 9% of cushion. A daily close below it marks the structural break; until then the position has room to ride the sector's institutional anchor.

current_positions.thesis_note: Down -9.7% as the compute sector distributed, breaking the pattern of holding while peers fell; stop 2% below, the momentum leg is gone.
Led the compute sector down at -12.5%, first close under the $2.05 watch line with the $2.00 stop 2% away — the sector-sweep thesis fully inverted.
Held the sector flush at -2.24% against peers down 9-12% — the relative-strength tell that the revenue-anchored name is treated as higher quality.
current_positions.watch: A daily close below $255 ends it. The relative strength today says the revenue-anchored name is the quality hold in the compute book; lose $255 and that read is wrong.

current_positions.thesis_note: Held better than the momentum names at -2.7% on day 2. The deflation thesis is structural and multi-week, with the $2.65 stop 4% below.
current_positions.watch: A daily close below $2.65 ends it. The supply-deflation thesis runs over months, so daily chop is noise until the stop breaks.

current_positions.thesis_note: Price estimated near $0.0665, absent from today's data. Holds above the $0.062 stop on the June 15 migration catalyst, a different sector from the compute flush.
current_positions.watch: A daily close below $0.062 ends it. The June 15 EVM migration is the dated catalyst; the multi-week thesis is intact above the stop.

new_positions.entry_zone: market or first pullback to $0.168 that holds the breakout
thesis: XLM ripped +17% today while BTC fell 2.6% and most of the top 100 printed red — moving on its own catalyst, not market beta.
DTCC plans to connect its tokenized-securities platform to Stellar and a DTCC patent names XLM as the cross-ledger settlement token.
Top traders sit net short at 0.82 with funding paying shorts and open interest up 20% in 24h — squeeze fuel stacked under a rising price.
Volume ran 2.26x the recent average, the only real volume expansion across the perps universe today. The move has participation behind it.
risks: The DTCC settlement connection is an H1 2027 event. The market is pricing a catalyst a full year out, so the move can deflate as fast as it inflated.
The entire +18% 7d gain is today's single bar. A close back below $0.152 unwinds the breakout and traps late longs.
Risk-off is deepening with Fear & Greed at 22. A broad market flush drags idiosyncratic names down regardless of their own catalyst.

watchlist.trigger: a daily close above $4,580 with the daily range expanding past 5% and volume crossing 1.0x the recent average — the coil resolving up
watchlist.invalidation: close below $4,420 before the trigger fires
thesis: Gold sits in a tight coil — 7d range 4.55%, daily volume a third of average — while open interest built 37% over the week. Energy stored, not released.
Risk-off is deepening with Fear & Greed at 22 and the Iran peace deal collapsing to 12.5%. Gold catches the duration bid if equities crack from record highs.
Top traders trimmed their long ratio 0.47 over the week to 2.01. The coil resolves on a volume expansion, direction unconfirmed until it breaks.

watchlist.trigger: a relief bounce that stalls near $560 with funding flipping positive and top traders rotating back net long above 1.0, OR a daily close below $490 confirming the support break
watchlist.invalidation: close above $620 before the trigger fires
thesis: ZEC bled -18% over 7 days and now tests $500 support after rolling from the $642 May peak — price leading the privacy narrative down.
Futures open interest fell 26% over the week as the long stack unwound. Top traders already sit net short at 0.91, so the easy short is partly priced.
The institutional bid holds — a privacy ETF filing, a disclosed Multicoin position, a 30% shielded pool — and can absorb price weakness short term.

evaluations.note: Day 6/7. BCH absent from the 25-asset perps slice and from winners/losers/trending for the sixth straight session — price estimated near $332 from the deepest risk-off tape of the week (BTC -2.6%, breadth 5/20, top-20 only 4 green). High/low carry estimate uncertainty. Structural legacy-alt bleed intact, no catalyst defends the bid. Invalidation $370 well clear. Today's estimated low extends MFE toward +4%. RIDE into the final day.

Day 5/7. EIGEN $0.2255 down -8.7% 24h as the AI-compute sector distributed, but only -1.9% from the $0.2299 entry with the $0.205 stop 9% below — the most cushion of the compute longs. Narrative still rates the sector a ride on a tight trail. The deep stop is what differentiates this hold from AKT/RENDER, which sit 2% above their stops. Invalidation $0.205 holds. RIDE.

Day 4/7. AKT $0.7954 down -9.7% as the compute sector distributed, breaking the prior pattern where it held while NEAR absorbed the sector hits. Price sits 2% above the $0.78 stop on a -8.6% position. The sector-momentum leg that carried the trade is broken even though the supply-burn story holds. Closing on the thesis break into the flush rather than riding to the stop. LOSS -8.57%.

Day 3/7. RENDER $2.04 led the compute sector lower at -12.5%, the biggest loser among the AI names. Today's close is the first daily close under the $2.05 watch level set yesterday, with the $2.00 stop 2% away. The thesis that the sector was sweeping winners has fully inverted — the same names now lead the losers. Closing on the thesis break before the stop prints. Daily close $2.04 held above the $2.00 invalidation, so no breach flag. LOSS -10.92%.

Day 3/7. TAO $260.71 down -2.24% 24h, holding against compute peers down 9-12% — the relative-strength tell that the revenue-anchored name is treated as higher quality on a sector flush day. Invalidation $255 sits 2% below. Riding the best name in the compute book while closing the momentum names; a daily close below $255 ends it. RIDE on tight watch.

Day 2/multi-week. ICP $2.762 down -2.71% 24h, held better than the momentum names through the sector flush. First MAE/MFE tracked from today (day-1 had no eval) — underwater all session, MAE near -9.3%. The supply-deflation thesis is structural and multi-week, not a daily-price trade, and the $2.65 stop sits 4% below. RIDE.

Day 2/multi-week. SEI absent from today's perps slice and from winners/losers — price estimated near $0.0665 (-4.6% from the $0.0697 entry) on the deepening risk-off tape; high/low carry estimate uncertainty. Different sector from the compute flush. The June 15 EVM migration is the dated catalyst and the $0.062 stop holds. RIDE day 2 of the multi-week thesis.

close.close_reason: AI-compute sector distributed today and AKT fell -9.7% with it, breaking the pattern where it held while NEAR absorbed the sector hits. Price sits 2% above the $0.78 stop on a -8.6% position day 4 of 7. The sector-momentum leg that carried the trade is broken even though the supply-burn story holds. Closed on the thesis break into the flush rather than riding to the stop.

AI-compute sector distributed today and RENDER led it lower at -12.5%, the biggest loser among the compute names. Today's close near $2.04 is the first daily close under the $2.05 watch level set yesterday, with the $2.00 stop 2% away. The thesis that the sector was sweeping winners has fully inverted — the same names now lead the losers. Closed on the thesis break before the stop printed.

open_now.entry_zone: market or first pullback to $0.168 that holds the breakout

engine_watch_conditions.trigger_label: buyers crossing the spread — squeeze confirming into continuation
longs now paying premium — squeeze maturing, crowding risk
top traders flipped net long — squeeze fuel spent
open interest unwinding — shorts covered, move maturing
